basics.name: Russell Batchelor
basics.headline: Infrastructure Programme Director | Multi-Site Transformation & Separation-Ready Delivery | Regulated UK/EMEA Estates | SC + NPPV3 Cleared

summary.title: Executive Summary
summary.content:
Infrastructure Programme Director with 20+ years delivering complex, multi-workstream transformation programmes across regulated UK and EMEA estates. Consistently engaged as the senior delivery lead where operational continuity is non-negotiable: live NHS sites, Tier 1 financial services, NATO-aligned infrastructure, central government and large-scale commercial environments. Brings deep technical literacy across LAN/Wi-Fi, SD-WAN/SASE, MPLS, data centre and EUC, combined with the governance discipline — RAID logs, change authority, dependency mapping, executive reporting — that keeps large programmes on track and compliant.

Equally comfortable directing a separation programme post-M&A, managing a 6,100-circuit MPLS migration across 130 sites, or standing up a greenfield £3M network from scratch in a live gaming estate. Operates effectively at Board and C-Suite level; known for producing clear, risk-aware governance packs that give senior stakeholders the right picture at the right time.

Available for contract engagements. SC Cleared and NPPV3 provide direct-access eligibility for regulated central government, defence, policing and NHS programmes without processing delay.

items.description:
• Facilitated AI discovery workshops for clients in legal and professional services, mapping current-state workflows to candidate AI automation use cases
  • Advised on Legal AI tooling integration, prompt engineering frameworks and governance guardrails appropriate for regulated environments
  • Designed and ran operational automation proof-of-concept exercises, identifying high-ROI workflow optimisation opportunities within programme management and reporting functions
  • Produced use case prioritisation matrices and implementation roadmaps for AI adoption at operational team level

items.position: Infrastructure Programme Manager, Live Estate Modernisation
items.description:
• Directing multi-workstream LAN/Wi-Fi/comms-room upgrade programme across live NHS estate, coordinating delivery around clinical schedules to achieve zero service disruption to patient-facing operations
  • Managing dependency sequencing across building phases, ensuring infrastructure readiness aligns with Windows 11 endpoint readiness milestones and EUC rollout timelines across approximately 5,000 staff
  • Orchestrating vendor delivery chain across network, cabling and facilities subcontractors; maintaining RAID log, change authority register and escalation governance
  • Producing executive programme reporting for NHS Trust and Atos stakeholders covering schedule, risk, change and service continuity assurance
  • Applying SC Cleared and NPPV3 credentials to enable direct access across secure NHS estate zones without vetting delay

• Leading network separation programme involving disentanglement of shared infrastructure following a structural change; coordinating logical and physical separation workstreams across LAN, WAN and security perimeters
  • Managing data centre transition workstreams including server, storage and connectivity rationalisation, with service continuity obligations governing cutover sequencing
  • Directing EUC upgrade programme across financial services estate, coordinating hardware refresh, image deployment and user communications to minimise business disruption
  • Maintaining full dependency management framework across concurrent workstreams; governing interdependencies between network separation, DC transition and EUC delivery milestones
  • Providing executive stakeholder reporting to client leadership covering programme status, risk register, change log and delivery confidence

items.position: Managing Director / Contract Infrastructure Programme Director
items.description:
• Own consultancy vehicle through which all contract programme and operations director engagements are delivered; each engagement places Russell as the senior delivery or operational lead inside the client organisation, accountable for programme outcomes, governance structures and stakeholder relationships
  • Provide independent programme assurance and advisory for client leadership teams, identifying root causes of delivery slippage, governance gaps, and operational risk; produce clear remediation plans with actionable recommendations
  • Lead discovery-phase assessments for new engagements, defining programme scope, delivery sequencing, governance structures, and resource requirements across complex multi-vendor environments
  • Review vendor performance and delivery capability against contractual commitments, challenging scope and sequencing assumptions on behalf of client programme boards

items.company: Las Vegas Sands (Major International Gaming Group)
items.description:
• Directed full programme lifecycle for greenfield network infrastructure: design authority, vendor selection, procurement, delivery management and commissioning of a £3M low-latency network serving 1,200+ gaming endpoints
  • Governed a multi-data-centre architecture spanning one primary data centre and seven IDCs, managing dependency sequencing, cutover planning and service continuity across all phases
  • Built and chaired Board-level KPI governance framework from programme inception: weekly service reviews and executive packs covering performance, risk, commercial position and compliance
  • Led a 24-engineer multilingual delivery and operations team across the full programme and transition to live service; managed cross-border logistics, vendor SLA compliance and escalation management
  • Designed the infrastructure roadmap, support structures and performance baselines that transitioned the programme into a sustainable, self-governing operational model at handover

• Directed 6,100+ circuit migrations across an MPLS-to-SASE transition programme serving regulated enterprise environments; managed cutover sequencing, dependency mapping and service-continuity obligations for live production networks across 130+ sites
  • Led infrastructure deployment programmes covering 7,000+ endpoints across LAN, Wi-Fi and SD-WAN environments; maintained full vendor orchestration across multi-supplier delivery chains in high-compliance settings
  • Governed NATO airbase Wi-Fi deployment across 200+ sites in 12+ nations: full dependency management, in-country logistics coordination, regulatory compliance alignment and executive programme reporting
  • Applied separation programme methodology to network and infrastructure workstreams in post-M&A and estate rationalisation contexts, coordinating logical network separation alongside physical estate changes
  • Produced C-Suite and Board-level governance packs covering RAID status, SLA performance, commercial position and programme assurance across all concurrent workstreams
  • Maintained SC Cleared and NPPV3 eligibility throughout, enabling direct engagement on secure government, defence and policing programme workstreams without vetting overhead

• Directed network separation and M&A integration workstreams across post-acquisition estate environments, coordinating logical and physical infrastructure disentanglement while maintaining service continuity across live production sites
  • Delivered 25,000 Wi-Fi endpoint deployment programme across a distributed enterprise estate, managing vendor orchestration, dependency sequencing and site-by-site cutover governance
  • Led seven data centre transition programmes, managing rack-and-stack, connectivity cutover, service migration sequencing and business sign-off at each phase
  • Applied infrastructure roadmap methodology to rationalise overlapping estate architectures inherited from M&A activity; produced executive programme reporting for client and internal leadership
  • Managed multi-supplier delivery chains across European geographies, coordinating installation, commissioning and handover against programme milestone governance

• Delivered GDS-aligned M365 transformation programmes including a 42,000-user Home Office migration, managing multi-workstream dependency mapping, cutover sequencing and stakeholder communications across a highly regulated central government environment
  • Provided infrastructure advisory and programme governance support across public sector clients, aligning delivery frameworks with GDS service standards, security requirements and change authority protocols
  • Managed vendor orchestration and subcontractor performance across complex multi-site delivery engagements in central government settings
  • Produced programme reporting and risk assurance documentation for senior government stakeholders and delivery boards

• Directed a full WAN infrastructure retender and network transformation programme, achieving a 60% reduction in WAN costs across the borough estate and securing a re-tendered contract worth £12M
  • Led infrastructure consolidation and digital transformation across 140+ council sites, managing multi-workstream delivery, vendor orchestration and dependency sequencing across a live public-sector estate
  • Governed the full programme lifecycle: infrastructure roadmap design, supplier selection, contract management, change authority and executive reporting to council leadership and elected members
  • Maintained service continuity across critical council functions — housing, benefits, planning — throughout infrastructure transition phases, applying zero-disruption delivery methodology in a politically sensitive public environment
  • Managed complex multi-supplier delivery chains across WAN, LAN, telephony and data centre workstreams, coordinating dependency management across simultaneous workstreams to programme milestone governance

items.name: Key Career Outcomes
items.description:
• 6,100+ circuit migrations | 7,000+ endpoints — Directed EMEA-wide MPLS-to-SASE transition portfolio across 130+ sites serving Tier 1 finance, NHS, UK Government and NATO-aligned clients, maintaining live service continuity throughout
  • NATO airbase Wi-Fi deployment | 200+ sites across 12+ nations — Programme Director for infrastructure standardisation across a NATO-aligned airbase estate; full dependency management, vendor orchestration and in-country logistics coordination
  • £3M greenfield network build | 1,200+ gaming endpoints | 7 IDCs — End-to-end programme and operations leadership for a first-of-kind low-latency network infrastructure in a live international gaming environment; Board KPI governance from Day 1
  • 60% WAN cost reduction | £12M re-tender — Led full ICT infrastructure retender and network transformation for a 5,000-employee public sector organisation across 140+ sites
  • 25,000 Wi-Fi endpoints | 7 data centre transitions — Infrastructure Project Director for M&A-driven estate transformations and network separation programmes across European multi-site enterprise environments

items.title: ITIL Foundation
items.issuer: ITIL-aligned programme delivery across NHS, Government, and Finance

items.content:
• Charles Stanley Group — Technology & Infrastructure Director (Contractor) | January 2013 – May 2015 — Infrastructure resilience, vendor governance and FCA-aligned IT service delivery for a regulated wealth management firm
  • Comunica — Operations Director (Contractor) | March 2011 – December 2012 — Pan-European telecoms and VoIP managed services; built and managed service operations across UK and European geographies
  • Ipitomi (Virgin Media Business) — Senior Operations Manager | January 2008 – February 2011 — Field service delivery and SLA governance across multi-site telecoms rollouts within a Virgin Media Business division
  • Whittington Insurance Markets (Lloyd's of London) — Head of IT & Network Operations | 2001 – 2007 — Full corporate IT infrastructure design and project management for a Lloyd's of London insurance group; led a team of 17 contract and permanent staff

Vehicle: Hiloka Limited (sole director) | Target rate: £800–£1,200/day (London), £600–£900/day (regions) — flexible for right-fit programme engagement | Availability: Within 2 weeks

Engagement type: SoW / output-based contracts | Location: UK-based; proven European delivery footprint | SC and NPPV3 cleared for regulated environments

jobContext.employer_name: Archetype A — Multi-Site Transformation & Separation-Ready Delivery
jobContext.job_description: Infrastructure Programme Director with 20+ years delivering multi-workstream transformation programmes across regulated UK and EMEA estates. SC and NPPV3 cleared. Target market: Enterprise IT Consultancies, MSPs, Government, Defence, Finance, NHS.
